skill.name: gate-dex-market
skill.description: Gate Wallet market data and token information queries for public on-chain data, including K-line data, liquidity, rankings, token details, new token discovery, and token risk information. It uses a Gate DEX MCP server and does not execute trades.
skill.summary: Query Gate DEX market data, token details, rankings, liquidity, and token risk information.
skill.author: gate

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command-execution and weak-cryptography patterns, but review found these are markdown code fences, inline tool names, example parameters, and data labels rather than executable code. The confirmed residual risks are intentional network use through a remote Gate MCP endpoint and instructions for editing local Cursor MCP configuration. No prompt injection attempt or confirmed malicious behavior was found.

medium_findings.title: External MCP Endpoint Used for Market Data
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to configure and call a remote Gate MCP service at api.gatemcp.ai. This appears intentional and aligned with the market-data purpose, but it means user queries and token addresses may be sent to an external service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The endpoint is explicitly documented in configuration examples and used by the required MCP workflow. The behavior is legitimate for this skill, but the network dependency is clear.

medium_findings.title: Local MCP Configuration Change Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill tells users to edit the Cursor MCP configuration under the home directory to add the Gate MCP server. This is not automatic file access, but it changes local AI tool configuration and should be reviewed by users before use.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file path and configuration block are explicit. Risk is limited because the skill provides instructions rather than executable code.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Alerts Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby backtick execution patterns are inline code, fenced examples, tool names, and response examples in markdown. I found no executable Ruby, shell script, or command launcher in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are documentation examples and markdown formatting. No evidence of executable command invocation was found in these files.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Cryptography Alerts Are Data-Label False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography alerts appear to match words such as kline, line, risk, or example fields inside documentation tables and examples. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, or custom cryptographic logic being implemented.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain descriptions, tables, and file structure text rather than cryptographic code. The scan signal does not correspond to an implemented algorithm.

low_findings.title: Raw MCP Error Display Could Expose Operational Details
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to display all MCP server errors as-is. This is useful for troubleshooting, but raw server errors can expose implementation details or sensitive diagnostics.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction is explicit, but actual exposure depends on how the remote MCP server formats errors. This is a minor semantic concern rather than confirmed leakage.

content.value_statement: Crypto market checks often require separate tools for price, liquidity, rankings, and contract risk. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to query Gate DEX public market data through MCP.
actual_capabilities: Checks Gate DEX MCP server availability before market workflows.
Retrieves K-line price data for a token on a supported chain.
Queries transaction statistics and pair liquidity for token market review.
Looks up token details such as supply, holders, price, website, and socials.
Ranks tokens by supported dimensions such as market cap, volume, change, or holders.
Queries token risk information and formats risk items for user review.
limitations: Requires a configured Gate DEX MCP server before use.
Does not execute swaps, transfers, approvals, or other on-chain writes.
Does not provide investment advice or decide whether a user should trade.
Data quality depends on the remote MCP service and available chain coverage.

use_cases.title: Token Market Review
use_cases.description: Check price movement, transaction activity, and liquidity before deeper manual research.

use_cases.title: Contract Risk Screening
use_cases.description: Request token risk information and see contract warnings before interacting with a token.

use_cases.title: New Token Discovery
use_cases.description: Find newly created tokens for a chain and prioritize follow-up checks by liquidity and risk.

prompt_templates.title: Check Token Details
prompt_templates.prompt: Look up token details for this contract on Ethereum: 0xdAC17F958D2ee523a2206206994597C13D831ec7.

prompt_templates.title: Review Market Activity
prompt_templates.prompt: Show the 24 hour K-line trend, transaction stats, and liquidity for this BSC token address.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Rankings
prompt_templates.prompt: List the top 10 Ethereum tokens by 24 hour volume and summarize the biggest movers.

prompt_templates.title: Screen a New Token
prompt_templates.prompt: Find new tokens created on BSC in the last 24 hours, then check risk info for the most active one.

output_examples.input: Show market data for a token address.
output_examples.output: A concise market summary with current price, 24 hour change, buy and sell counts, liquidity, main pairs, and suggested follow-up checks.

output_examples.input: Check token risk for a contract.
output_examples.output: A risk report showing contract address, chain, risk level, audit status, verification status, owner status, and listed risk items.
A short ranked list of new tokens with creation time, price, market cap, holders, and a reminder to review contract risk.

best_practices: Verify the MCP server URL and organization before sending market queries.
Use contract addresses when possible because token symbols can be ambiguous.
Pair market data with risk information before drawing conclusions.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the output as financial advice or a trading recommendation.
Do not use the skill to execute trades, approvals, transfers, or wallet actions.
Do not assume a low risk result means a token is safe to buy.

faq.question: Does this skill require authentication?
faq.answer: The documented market tools use public queries and do not require an MCP token.

faq.question: Can this skill trade tokens?
faq.answer: No. It is read-only and is not designed for swaps, transfers, approvals, or signing.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What data can it retrieve?
faq.answer: It can retrieve K-line data, transaction stats, liquidity, token details, rankings, new token lists, and token risk information.

faq.question: Why does it need an MCP server?
faq.answer: The skill is an instruction layer for Gate DEX MCP tools, so the server must be configured before use.

faq.question: Is the security audit output a guarantee?
faq.answer: No. It reports available risk data and should be combined with independent review.
